pages.text: Novus draws its indoor furniture from a small constellation of mods — most of all $(italic)Another Furniture$(), with quiet additions from elsewhere. The chairs sit, the tables join, the drawers store. Vanilla blocks plus what follows give you a furnished room.
pages.title: Furnishings

pages.text: $(bold)Seats that sit.$() Five sittable pieces in every overworld wood: $(bold)Chair$(), $(bold)Bench$(), $(bold)Sofa$() (dyed, multi-seat), $(bold)Stool$(), and $(bold)Tall Stool$() (for the kitchen counter).$(br2)Right-click to climb on. Shift to climb off. A sofa or bench will seat several players at once.
pages.title: Seats

pages.text: $(bold)Tables.$() One table per wood; place two adjacent and they join into a single communal slab. Continue along a wall and the table runs the whole length, with shaped corners where it turns.
pages.title: Tables

pages.text: $(bold)Storage.$()$(br)$(li)$(bold)Drawer$() — a chest at half the height. Same nine-row UI; no head-banging when stacked beneath shelves.$(li)$(bold)Shelf$() — a 1×1 display block. Right-click with an item and it appears in the model. Three slots.$(br2)Vanilla chests and barrels still work as ever; drawers and shelves are for the rooms where they would feel oversized.
pages.title: Storage
